title: Quiz: Entropic Inequalities
description: Test your understanding of entropic inequalities

questions.question: Which inequality characterizes the 2-party quantum entropy cone?
questions.explanation: For 2 parties, the cone is characterized by non-negativity, subadditivity S(AB) ≤ S(A) + S(B), and Araki-Lieb |S(A)-S(B)| ≤ S(AB).

questions.question: All classical entropic inequalities also hold for quantum systems.
questions.explanation: False. Some classical inequalities (like non-negativity of conditional entropy) fail quantumly.

questions.question: The quantum entropy cone is:

options.text: A strict subset of the classical cone
A strict superset of the classical cone

questions.explanation: Neither contains the other: quantum allows negative conditional entropy (outside classical), but classical satisfies some inequalities quantum doesn't.

questions.question: For 3+ party systems, the entropy cone is:
questions.explanation: For 4+ parties, the quantum entropy cone is not fully characterized. Unknown inequalities may exist beyond SSA.
